channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

Toilet tank lid. Unhygienic and it needs to be removed.

item.title: Flipped mattress, 2nd May
item.description:
Abandoned mattress on side of the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crowhurst Close, Loughborough Junction, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pedestrian crossing near the Portuguese Deli., 2nd May
item.description:
The majority of motorists are failing to stop at the pedestrian crossing. I have witnessed some near misses of the public almost being hit by cars and bikes. I have also experienced this myself on numerous occasions. Cars do not stop. This must be due to the road markings being very worn and difficult to see. The paint appears both very faded and dirty, reducing visibility for drivers and pedestrians. It seems that the pedestrian crossing is not being “seen” in time. This presents a safety concern. Please could the crossing be inspected and repainted. Surely, pedestrian crossings generally are routinely checked and maintained by the council.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 36, Delancey Street, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kerbside grid on Heathwood Gardens blocked, 2nd May
item.description:
Located outside 11 Heathwood Gardens this grid/drain is completely blocked by building debris, brick dust and cement leaving any rainfall to pass over the grid and down the road. Attempts by neighbours to shift debris with garden tools have been ineffective. Been like this for at least 3 years.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Heathwood Gardens, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 2nd May
item.description:
Sanford Street. Remove graffiti from bus shelter at stop A

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sanford Street, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: The tree appears to be half dead., 2nd May
item.description:
The tree has a lot of mistletoe and the top branches appear dead and there are not many leaves on the lower branches. Branches frequently fall into footpath and our garden wall is by the tree and we are concerned the tree will fall and damage the wall. The footpath where the tree is located is well used and near a school and there is a danger to pedestrians. I think it needs either cutting right back or removing.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hayes Lane, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over 31mm pothole, 2nd May
item.description:
At sod of number 10This pothole meets the criteria whole section needs resurfacing. why did you stop at No 10.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Priestlands Park Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti needs cleaning, 2nd May

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 90, High Street, Goddington, Orpington, Greater London

item.title: Fly tipped rubbish on pavement, 2nd May
item.description:
Lots of household and commercial rubbish on the side of the street and pavement - more added often

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Waylett Place, West Norwood, Greater London

item.title: Light to bright, 2nd May
item.description:
The street light outside my property is very very bright. I would like to have one of the cover put up to avoid it shining into my bedroom as this is keeping me awake.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 92, The Drive, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
